---
title: "Project 1 Final Report"
---

| | |
|---|---|
| **Project** | Project 1: Linear Regression |
| **Due** | Week 12 |
| **Weight** | 40% of Project 1 (which is 20% of final grade) |
| **Related Guide** | [Project Guide]({{< var books.project_guide >}}){target="_blank"} |

::: {.callout-tip}
## {{< fa book >}} Project Guide
Refer to the [Project Guide]({{< var books.project_guide >}}){target="_blank"} for the complete rubric, formatting guidelines, and examples of polished final reports.
:::

## Purpose

The final report is a polished, complete linear regression analysis that demonstrates your ability to formulate a research question, select and fit appropriate models, diagnose problems, and communicate statistical findings clearly. This submission should incorporate all feedback received on your proposal and draft.

## Requirements

Your final report should include all of the following sections:

### 1. Introduction

- Clear statement of the research question
- Motivation and context for the analysis
- Overview of the analytical approach

### 2. Data and Variables

- Complete dataset description with source and sample information
- Variable definitions, coding, and measurement scales
- Descriptive statistics (tables and/or figures)

### 3. Exploratory Data Analysis

- Visualizations of key relationships (scatterplots, boxplots, etc.)
- Discussion of patterns, outliers, and potential issues

### 4. Model Specification and Results

- Clearly stated regression model(s)
- Complete regression output with coefficient estimates, standard errors, *t*-statistics, and $p$-values
- Model fit statistics ($R^2$, adjusted $R^2$, residual standard error)
- Substantive interpretation of all key coefficients
- Hypothesis tests for research questions

### 5. Model Diagnostics

- Residual analysis (residuals vs. fitted, Q-Q plot, scale-location plot)
- Assessment of regression assumptions
- Influential observations analysis (Cook's distance, leverage)
- Any remedial actions taken

### 6. Discussion and Conclusion

- Summary of key findings in relation to the research question
- Limitations of the analysis
- Suggestions for future research

## Formatting

- Use clear headings and subheadings
- Number all tables and figures with descriptive captions
- Report statistics using proper notation
- Length: approximately 8--12 pages (excluding code appendix)

## Submission

Submit the following to Blackboard:

- A rendered PDF or HTML document
- Your `.qmd` source file
- The dataset (or a link to it)
